element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Used to eliminate ghosts throughout game
Touchable hazards in Zuul Building; harmless outside
Money earned from ghost elimination funds purchases

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Available in shop after earning currency

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Must be eliminated to progress to boss

element_introduction_order.element: Boss enemies (Stay Puft Marshmallow Man, others)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Must be defeated and captured to progress
Pieces retrieved from bosses unlock castle level
Unlocked only after collecting all tablet pieces

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Scattered in levels, available in shop
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: HP replenishment for sustaining boss fights

typical_tension_triggers: Touching ghosts in Zuul Building (causes fall)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Repetitive wave-based encounters (middle ghosts) leading to escalating boss difficulty; notorious difficulty spike in staircase sequence prevents many players from reaching final boss

difficulty_curve.early_game: Accessible introduction with basic platforming and ghost elimination mechanics; shop system teaches resource management
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalating boss difficulty; middle ghost waves increase in challenge requiring upgraded weapons for effective progression
difficulty_curve.late_game: Notorious difficulty spike at staircase sequence in Zuul Building; acts as hard gate preventing majority of players from reaching final boss; final battle only accessible to those who overcome this extreme challenge
notable_spikes: Staircase sequence in Zuul Building (described as 'super-hard and grueling')
Transition from 4 optional-order levels to mandatory castle level
Final boss battle accessibility (locked behind staircase gatekeep)

notable_level_patterns.description: Four haunted residential building levels can be completed in any order before unlocking castle stage; allows player agency in difficulty progression via stone tablet collection
Middle ghosts appear mid-level and must be eliminated before boss can be engaged; creates tension/relief cycle within single level
Each boss must be defeated then captured; adds post-victory action requirement and narrative progression (stone tablet collection)
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All levels ending with boss fights

notable_level_patterns.description: Staircase sequence acts as hard gate blocking access to final content; design prevents majority of players from reaching ending
Between-level sequences requiring vehicle avoidance; pacing breaks between platforming sections (1 vehicle in US version, 2 in JP)

sources_used: tcrf - The Cutting Room Floor (Ghostbusters NES article)

wikipedia - Ghostbusters (1990 video game) - Note: Wikipedia article describes Sega Genesis version, not NES version, but provides gameplay structure details applicable to understanding platformer design paradigms
